title: Center-fixed mems microphone membrane
abstract: A MEMS microphone (101) having a backplate (105), a spring (215), and a membrane (103). In one embodiment, the membrane (103) is supported in an approximate center of the membrane via a support (107). The support (107) is connected to the approximate center of the membrane (103) and an approximate center of the backplate (105). The membrane (103) is connected to a spring (215) that provides an electrical connection. The membrane (103) may be electrically biased via the electrical connection. In a different embodiment, one or more overtravel stops (617) are fixed to the backplate (605) and pass through an aperture (619) of the membrane (603). The overtravel stops (617) are configured to prevent movement of the membrane (603) in a radial direction opposite to the backplate (605). The membrane (603) includes a stress gradient, a corrugation, or another structure that sets or determines a stiffness of the membrane.
claims: Claims What is claimed is:  Claims: 1. A micro-electromechanical system (MEMS) microphone comprising: a membrane having a predetermined stiffness and a central region; at least one spring, the at least one spring having a first end and a second end, the first end connected to the membrane; a backplate; a support connected to the central region of the membrane and connected to the backplate, the support positioned between the membrane and the backplate. 2. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the membrane has an approximate center and the approximate center is positionally fixed relative to the backplate by the support. 3. The MEMS microphone of claim 2, wherein the approximate center of the membrane is at a geographical center of the membrane. 4. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the backplate includes perforations that are configured to allow acoustic pressure to pass through the backplate and to the membrane. 5. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the membrane is configured to flex around the support when the membrane receives incident acoustic pressure. 6. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the membrane includes a surface and corrugations positioned on the surface, the corrugations setting the predetermined stiffness of the membrane. 7. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the membrane includes at least one selected from the group consisting of ribs, circle beams, spots, and spokes. 8. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, further comprising an overtravel stop connected to the backplate, the overtravel stop extending through an aperture in the membrane. 9. The MEMS microphone of claim 8, wherein the overtravel stop includes a first end fixed to the backplate, a structure extending through the aperture of the membrane, and a second end positioned on an opposite side of the membrane relative to the first end, the second end configured to stop movement of the membrane in a radial direction. 10. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the second end of the at least one spring is connected to the backplate. 11. The MEMS microphone of claim 1 comprising only a single spring. 12. The MEMS microphone of claim 1, wherein the at least one spring is formed from a slice of the membrane along a portion of a perimeter of the membrane. 13. A method of limiting movement of a membrane of a MEMS microphone, the MEMS microphone including a backplate, an overtravel stop and a spring, the method comprising: coupling a first end of the spring to the membrane; coupling a center region of the membrane via a support to a center region of the backplate; and positioning an overtravel stop through an aperture in the membrane. 14. The method of claim 13, the method further comprising: affixing a first end of the overtravel stop to the backplate; positioning a second end of the overtravel stop on an opposite side of the membrane relative to the first end; and when the membrane moves a predetermined distance in a radial direction opposite the backplate, contacting the second end of the overtravel stop. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein coupling a center region of the membrane via a support includes forming a support in a substrate and directly coupling the center region of the membrane to the backplate via the support.
